questions.text: Which of the following hydrogen halide is the weakest acid in solution:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The bond strength H-X decreases from HF to HI. Thus HF is the most stable and hence weakest acid while HI is least stable and most acidic. In aqueous solutions HF is only slightly ionised but HCl HBr and HI are almost completely ionised.

Substance that releases H+(hydrogen-ion) and can easily dissociate completely in aqueous solution, is a strong acid. And HBr breaks off completely in water, making itself a strong acid. In aqueous solutions HF is only slightly ionised but HCl HBr and HI are almost completely ionised.

questions.text: Hydrogen bonding is the strongest among the molecules of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Despite its electronegativity, size of a chlorine atom is larger and hence, the electron density is low. So, HCl does not have hydrogen bonding but, HF,NH3,H2O shows hydrogen bonding.
The stronger hydrogen bond is formed between fluorine and hydrogen due to the highest electronegativity of fluorine.

Hydrogen bonding is a special type of dipole-dipole attraction between molecules, not a covalent bond to a hydrogen atom. It results from the attractive force between a hydrogen atom covalently bonded to a very electronegative atom such as a N, O, or F atom and another very electronegative atom.

Hydrogen bonding is a special type of dipole-dipole attraction between molecules, not a covalent bond to a hydrogen atom. It results from the attractive force between a hydrogen atom covalently bonded to a very electronegative atom such as a N, O, or F atom and another very electronegative atom.

questions.explanation: The stronger hydrogen bond is formed between fluorine and hydrogen due to the highest electronegativity of fluorine.

questions.text: Which of the following halogen is in solid form at room temperature:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Iodine (melting point 114oC) exists as solid at room temperature. Astatine (Melting point 302oC) also exists as a solid at room temperature.
Fluorine is a naturally-occurring, pale yellow-green gas with a sharp odor.
Bromine is a naturally occurring element that is a liquid at room temperature. It has a brownish-red color with a bleach-like odor, and it dissolves in water.
Elemental chlorine is a pale, yellowy green gas at room temperature.

questions.explanation: Iodine (melting point 114oC) exists as solid at room temperature. Astatine (Melting point 302oC) also exists as a solid at room temperature.

questions.text: Which of the halogens is most easily reduced:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Iodine has the highest electrode potential value and thus is the weakest oxidising agent or the strongest reducing agent.
Br- loses an electron; it is oxidized from Br- to Br2; thus, Br- is the reducing agent. Cl2 gains one electron; it is reduced from Cl2 to 2 Cl-; thus, Cl2 is the oxidizing agent.
Fluorine has the highest reduction potential and is therefore the most easily reduced halogen.
Br- loses an electron; it is oxidized from Br- to Br2; thus, Br- is the reducing agent. Cl2 gains one electron; it is reduced from Cl2 to 2 Cl-; thus, Cl2 is the oxidizing agent.

questions.explanation: Fluorine has the highest reduction potential and is therefore the most easily reduced halogen.

questions.text: Which silver salt is sparingly soluble in H2O:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Unlike the other silver halides, AgF is highly soluble in water (1800 g/L), and it even has some solubility in acetonitrile. It is also unique among silver(I) compounds and the silver halides in that it forms the hydrates AgF·(H2O)2 and AgF·(H2O)4 on precipitation from aqueous solution.
Silver bromide (AgBr) is a soft, pale-yellow, water-insoluble salt well known (along with other silver halides) for its unusual sensitivity to light.
Silver chloride is sparingly soluble in water because its lattice energy is greater than its hydration energy.

Solid silver iodide is preferred as a nucleator for artificially stimulated rain because its crystal lattice parameters are close to those of ice, and for that reason are more effective than other salts in causing small ice crystals in clouds to melt.

questions.explanation: It is well established in the pedagogical literature that AgCl is insoluble in water while NaCl and KCl are soluble: applications of this difference are made in elementary studies of both qualitative and quantitative analysis. What is usually left unsaid, however, is why AgCl is so much less soluble than NaCl or KCl.

questions.text: All the halogens are coloured. The intensity of the colour depends on:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The colour of halogens is due to the absorption of different quanta of radiations in the visible region which results in excitation of outer electrons to higher energy levels, thus different colours are observed.﻿The colour of halogens progressively deepens from fluorine to iodine because: halogens of higher atomic number absorb light of longer wavelength since the difference in energy between the ground state and excited state decreases as the atomic number increases.

questions.text: Consider the following reaction the type of above reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Bleaching action occurs through oxidation or reduction. Chlorine bleaches work through oxidation; they break the chemical bond of the chromophore (a color-producing portion of pigment) rendering it non-reactive with light. Reductions convert double bonds to single bonds, again making them non-reactive to visible light.

A single-displacement reaction, also known as single replacement reaction or exchange reaction, is an archaic concept in chemistry. It describes the stoichiometry of some chemical reactions in which one element or ligand is replaced by atom or group.

A decomposition reaction occurs when one reactant breaks down into two or more products. This can be represented by the general equation: AB → A + B. • Examples of decomposition reactions include the breakdown of hydrogen peroxide to water and oxygen, and the breakdown of water to hydrogen and oxygen.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in radiotherapy:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Radon is used in radiotherapy. Radon is a noble gas but still radioactive.

The new approach of this invention is to administer radiotherapy in the form of the radioactive gas Xenon-133, which is infused through a catheter directly into the core of the tumor. This method of radiation therapy allows for a major improvement in target to non-target radiation over external beam X-ray therapy.

Krypton is used commercially as a filling gas for energy-saving fluorescent lights. It is also used in some flash lamps used for high-speed photography. Unlike the lighter gases in its group, it is reactive enough to form some chemical compounds.

Argon is often used when an inert atmosphere is needed. It is used in this way for the production of titanium and other reactive elements. It is also used by welders to protect the weld area and in incandescent light bulbs to stop oxygen from corroding the filament.

questions.explanation: Radon is used in radiotherapy. Radon is a noble gas but still radioactive.

questions.text: Who reported the first noble gas:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: He discovered the nature and properties of hydrogen, the specific heat of certain substances, and various properties of electricity. He measured the density and mass of the Earth by the method now known as the Cavendish experiment.

Frankland introduced the theory of valence when he realised that atoms can only form a certain number of bonds to other atoms. He also co-discovered helium (He), pioneered organometallic chemistry, and was a leader in the field of water purification.

The first noble gas discovered by Ramsay was argon and the discovery of fluorine by Moissan preceded it by 9 years only. When Ramsay observed the inertness of argon, he sent a sample to Moissan who mixed it with fluorine and passed the reaction mixture through an electric discharge.

Rayleigh scattering, dispersion of electromagnetic radiation by particles that have a radius less than approximately 1/10 the wavelength of the radiation. The process has been named in honour of Lord Rayleigh, who in 1871 published a paper describing this phenomenon.

questions.text: Which noble gas does not obey octet rule:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: They are already born with 8 electrons, generally, so they already obey the octet rule without bonding. The simple logic is that 8 + 0 = 8 and they won't bond. The figure above shows neon (Ne) surrounded by 4 pairs of dots, without any bonds. The same would apply for Ar, Kr, and Xe.

The octet rule states that atoms tend to form compounds in ways that give them eight valence electrons, and thus the electron configuration of a noble gas. An exception to an octet of electrons is in the case of the first noble gas, helium, which only has two valence electrons.

questions.text: Chlorine heptaoxide (Cl2O7) reacts with water to form:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When nonmetals react with water, oxoacids are produced. Oxoacids are acids that contain a central atom with the same oxidation state as the corresponding acidic oxide. The oxidation state of chlorine in is +7. This means we need 1 hydrogen and 4 oxygen atoms in the acid to produce a neutral molecule (since hydrogen has an oxidation state of +1, and oxygen -2, which yields a net -7 charge that is balanced by chlorine's +7).

options.explanation: Chlorine ( Cl2 ) is the halogen react spontaneously with gold to produce gold ions.Halogens react with other non-metals to form covalent compounds. For example, hydrogen reacts with chlorine to form hydrochloric acid (HCl).

questions.text: Bleaching powder may be produced by passing chlorine over:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Bleaching powder is prepared by passing chlorine gas over dry slaked lime.

questions.text: Which is the strongest acid:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: HClO4 (perchloric acid) is a stronger acid than HClO3 (chloric acid). In fact, HClO4 is generally regarded the strongest of all known simple acids. Both HClO4 and HClO3 are oxyacids of chlorine, and the oxidation number of chlorine in them are +7 and +5 respectively.
The order of acidic strength of HOCl,HClO2,HClO3 and HClO4 are: HClO4>HClO3>HClO2>HClO.
The order of acidic strength of HOCl,HClO2,HClO3 and HClO4 are: HClO4>HClO3>HClO2>HClO.

HClO4 (perchloric acid) is a stronger acid than HClO3 (chloric acid). In fact, HClO4 is generally regarded the strongest of all known simple acids. Both HClO4 and HClO3 are oxyacids of chlorine, and the oxidation number of chlorine in them are +7 and +5 respectively.

questions.explanation: HClO4 (perchloric acid) is a stronger acid than HClO3 (chloric acid). In fact, HClO4 is generally regarded the strongest of all known simple acids. Both HClO4 and HClO3 are oxyacids of chlorine, and the oxidation number of chlorine in them are +7 and +5 respectively.

questions.text: Which halogen occurs naturally in a positive oxidation state:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fluorine is assigned the oxidation number of -1 because it attracts the electrons in the bond more strongly than the carbon does. Thus, fluorine appears to have an extra electron, -1 oxidation number. F is the most electronegative element on the periodic table.
Although chlorine usually has a -1 oxidation state, it can have oxidation states of +1, +3, +4, or +7 in certain compounds, such as when it forms oxoacids with the alkali metals.

The most stable oxidation state of the element is −1, in which bromine occurs naturally. But oxidation states of 0 (elemental bromine, Br2), +1 (hypobromite, BrO−), +3 (bromite, BrO−2), +5 (bromate, BrO−3), and +7 (perbromate, BrO−4) are also known.

Depends on the theme of the question, so if you are asking for the halogen which has a positive oxidation state in its naturally occurring minerals, then the answer is Iodine since it has the most positive oxidation state in most of its natural mineral sources.

questions.explanation: Depends on the theme of the question, so if you are asking for the halogen which has a positive oxidation state in its naturally occurring minerals, then the answer is Iodine since it has the most positive oxidation state in most of its natural mineral sources.

questions.text: An element that has a high ionization energy and tends to be chemically inactive would most likely to be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Since the reactivity of alkali metals increases on moving down the Group, Francium is the most reactive alkali metal followed by Caesium, Potassium, Sodium, and Lithium.

Compared with the alkali metals in group 1 and the alkaline Earth metals in group 2, the transition metals are much less reactive. They don't react quickly with water or oxygen, which explains why they resist corrosion. Other properties of the transition metals are unique.

Noble gases have the largest first ionization potential among the elements of each period, which reflects the stability of their electron configuration and is related to their relative lack of chemical reactivity.

Halogens are found in Group 7 of the periodic table meaning that they have 7 electrons in their outermost shell. This means that when they react, they gain one electron to achieve a full outer shell like noble gases. The reactivity of halogens decreases as you move down Group 7.

questions.explanation: Noble gases have the largest first ionization potential among the elements of each period, which reflects the stability of their electron configuration and is related to their relative lack of chemical reactivity.

questions.text: Which of the following represents the correct electronic configuration of the outermost energy level of an element of zero (VIIIA) group in the ground state:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Its electron configuration is 1s22s22p63s1. The first ten electrons of the sodium atom are the inner-shell electrons and the configuration of just those ten electrons is exactly the same as the configuration of the element neon (Z = 10).
